summary: Dan Shapiro recommends a niche woodworking website's 'Wood of the Month' feature from a trade magazine he discovered at a tool sale. He notes the magazine is narrowly targeted at high-volume professional woodworkers and mostly not relevant to him, but finds the wood profiles interesting. He also mentions the publisher offers free subscriptions to subtly different magazine variants for different woodworking specialties.
key_points: Recommends the 'Wood of the Month' section from iswonline, a niche woodworking trade publication
Found the magazine at the Grizzly Tent Sale, a woodworking tool event
Notes the publisher segments nearly identical content into slightly different trade magazine variants
money_quotes: A magazine that you probably haven't read produces a site that you probably haven't found.
They don't want to let you sign up for all of them, probably because they're all quite similar.
key_insight: An early glimpse into Shapiro's maker culture interests, sharing a niche woodworking resource years before founding laser-cutter company Glowforge.
